title: Fix sl init on Windows (embed.FS path separator bug)
description: sl init and sl new fail on Windows because filepath.Join produces backslash paths for embed.FS lookups. embed.FS requires forward slashes on all platforms. Results in: playbook copying failed, manifest not found, playbook name required errors.

title: Foundational: Fix embed.FS path separators in manifest and playbook lookup
description: filepath.Join produces OS-native separators (backslash on Windows) but embed.FS requires forward slashes. Fixing manifest.go and embedded.go unblocks all other user story work.

items.item: embedded.go uses path.Join for both embed.FS lookups
path/filepath import removed where no longer needed

design: Fix manifest.go:12: filepath.Join(templatesDir, manifest.yaml) -> path.Join(...). Fix embedded.go:90: filepath.Join(s.templatesDir, manifest.yaml) -> path.Join(...). Fix embedded.go:97: filepath.Join(s.templatesDir, pb.Path) -> path.Join(...). Import: replace path/filepath with path in both files.
acceptance_criteria: LoadManifest succeeds on Windows. ValidatePlaybooks finds manifest and playbook paths. No filepath import remains in manifest.go or embedded.go.

title: Fix manifest.go: use path.Join for embed.FS manifest path
description: WHY: filepath.Join produces backslash on Windows; embed.FS requires forward slash. manifest.go:12 constructs the manifest path with filepath.Join causing file-not-found on Windows.
design: File: pkg/cli/playbooks/manifest.go. Line 12: change filepath.Join(templatesDir, manifest.yaml) to path.Join(templatesDir, manifest.yaml). Update import: remove path/filepath, add path. Verify no other filepath usages remain in this file.
acceptance_criteria: LoadManifest("templates") succeeds and returns valid manifest with at least one playbook. No filepath import in manifest.go.

title: Fix embedded.go: use path.Join for ValidatePlaybooks embed.FS paths
description: WHY: embedded.go:90 and :97 use filepath.Join to build paths for embed.FS Exists checks. On Windows these produce backslash paths which embed.FS does not recognize.
design: File: pkg/cli/playbooks/embedded.go. Line 90: filepath.Join(s.templatesDir, manifest.yaml) -> path.Join(...). Line 97: filepath.Join(s.templatesDir, pb.Path) -> path.Join(...). Update import: remove path/filepath, add path. Verify no other filepath usages remain.
acceptance_criteria: ValidatePlaybooks() returns nil error when embedded FS contains the manifest and specledger playbook. No filepath import in embedded.go.

title: US1: sl init completes successfully on Windows
description: After the foundational manifest fix, copy.go and bootstrap_helpers.go still use filepath.Join for embed.FS paths used in walk comparisons and init.sh lookup. These cause playbook files to not be copied and init.sh to not be found.

items.item: No Playbook copying failed in output
No playbook name is required error

design: Fixes in copy.go (srcPath, relPath calculation) and bootstrap_helpers.go:449 (init.sh path). See plan.md Fix 3 and Fix 4a for exact line numbers and diffs.
acceptance_criteria: sl init exits code 0 on Windows. specledger.yaml is created with playbook name specledger. No Playbook copying failed warning in output. No playbook name is required error.

title: Fix copy.go: use path.Join and strings.TrimPrefix for embed.FS walk paths
description: WHY: copy.go:19 builds srcPath with filepath.Join. On Windows this produces a backslash path. The walk paths from embed.FS always use forward slashes, so strings.HasPrefix and filepath.Rel comparisons fail, causing all playbook files to be skipped.

items.item: path.Join used for srcPath at line 19
filepath import kept (still used for destPath)

design: File: pkg/cli/playbooks/copy.go. Line 19: filepath.Join(srcDir, playbook.Path) -> path.Join(srcDir, playbook.Path). Add path import. Line 57: remove filepath.Rel(srcPath, path) and replace with: relPath := strings.TrimPrefix(path, srcPath+"/"). This works because line 52 already guarantees the HasPrefix condition. Remove the surrounding error block for filepath.Rel since it is no longer needed. Keep filepath import for lines 83,97 (real filesystem paths).
acceptance_criteria: CopyPlaybooks copies all playbook files to destination. result.FilesCopied > 0 after copy. relPath is correctly computed as relative path from srcPath.

title: Fix bootstrap_helpers.go: use path.Join for init.sh embed.FS path
description: WHY: bootstrap_helpers.go:449 uses filepath.Join("templates", playbookName, "init.sh") to construct an embed.FS path. On Windows this produces templates\specledger\init.sh which TemplatesFS.ReadFile cannot find.

items.item: filepath import preserved (still used for real FS paths)

design: File: pkg/cli/commands/bootstrap_helpers.go. Line 449: filepath.Join("templates", playbookName, "init.sh") -> path.Join("templates", playbookName, "init.sh"). Add "path" to imports. Verify filepath import is still used elsewhere in this file (it is, for real FS paths).
acceptance_criteria: runPostInitScript finds init.sh in embedded FS on Windows. No script-not-found silent failure for the init.sh lookup.

title: Add unit test: LoadManifest uses forward-slash paths (cross-platform)
description: WHY: Regression guard for the manifest.go path separator fix. Without this test, a future developer could accidentally reintroduce filepath.Join without the build failing on macOS/Linux.
design: File: pkg/cli/playbooks/manifest_test.go (new file). Test: TestLoadManifestPathForwardSlash. Call LoadManifest("templates") and assert no error and at least one playbook returned. This test exercises the real embed.FS so it catches the separator issue on any platform. Also test TestManifestPathNeverHasBackslash: verify path.Join("templates", "manifest.yaml") == "templates/manifest.yaml" (not backslash).
acceptance_criteria: TestLoadManifestPathForwardSlash passes on Windows, macOS, Linux. Test fails if filepath.Join is reintroduced.

title: Add unit test: embed.FS path separator in copy.go (cross-platform)
description: WHY: Regression guard for copy.go srcPath fix. Verifies embed.FS paths must use forward slashes and that backward-slash paths do not resolve.

items.item: Forward slash Exists check returns true

design: File: pkg/cli/playbooks/copy_test.go (new file or add to existing). Test: TestEmbedFSPathSeparator. Assert Exists("templates/specledger") == true. Assert Exists("templates\specledger") == false. This will catch any future regression to filepath.Join for embed.FS paths.
acceptance_criteria: TestEmbedFSPathSeparator passes on all platforms. Forward slash path resolves; backslash path does not.

title: Extend bootstrap_test.go: assert no playbook failure warnings in sl init
description: WHY: Existing integration test only checks exit code and metadata but not output content. A Windows user would see playbook failure warnings even if the command succeeds. This assertion closes that gap.

items.item: Assertion added to TestBootstrapInitInExistingDirectory for Playbook copying failed
Assertion added for playbook name is required

design: File: tests/integration/bootstrap_test.go. In TestBootstrapInitInExistingDirectory: after cmd.CombinedOutput(), add: if strings.Contains(string(output), "Playbook copying failed") { t.Errorf(...) }. Also add check for "playbook name is required". Ensure strings import is present.
acceptance_criteria: TestBootstrapInitInExistingDirectory fails if sl init prints any playbook-related warning. Test passes after all fixes are applied.

title: US2: Tool availability detection and post-init script work on Windows
description: checkGum() uses command -v (Unix shell built-in, unavailable on Windows as executable) and runPostInitScript executes a .sh file directly (Windows cannot execute shell scripts without an interpreter).

items.item: runPostInitScript detects Windows and finds shell or skips gracefully
No panic or unhandled error from these code paths on Windows

design: Fix terminal.go checkGum. Fix bootstrap_helpers.go runPostInitScript with Windows shell detection. See plan.md Fix 4b and Fix 5.
acceptance_criteria: checkGum() returns correct result on Windows. sl init does not crash or show unexpected errors from post-init phase on Windows without Git Bash. sl init uses bash.exe/sh.exe if available on Windows.

title: Fix terminal.go checkGum: use exec.LookPath instead of exec.Command("command")
description: WHY: exec.Command("command", "-v", "gum") fails on Windows because "command" is a bash/sh built-in, not a standalone executable. exec.LookPath is the idiomatic cross-platform way to check for tool availability.
design: File: pkg/cli/tui/terminal.go. Replace checkGum() body: remove exec.Command("command", "-v", "gum").Run() == nil. Replace with: _, err := exec.LookPath("gum"); return err == nil. exec import already present, no changes needed to imports.
acceptance_criteria: checkGum() returns false when gum is not installed (on any OS). checkGum() returns true when gum is in PATH (on any OS). No exec.Command("command"...) call remains.

title: Fix runPostInitScript: add Windows shell detection for .sh execution
description: WHY: exec.Command(tmpFile.Name()) on a .sh file fails on Windows because Windows cannot execute shell scripts without an interpreter. The fix detects if a Unix shell (bash/sh) is available via Git for Windows and uses it; otherwise skips the post-init script gracefully.

items.item: Graceful skip when no shell found on Windows

design: File: pkg/cli/commands/bootstrap_helpers.go. After tmpFile.Close() (around line 476), add: var cmd *exec.Cmd; if runtime.GOOS == "windows" { shell := findWindowsShell(); if shell == "" { return }; cmd = exec.Command(shell, tmpFile.Name()) } else { cmd = exec.Command(tmpFile.Name()) }. Add findWindowsShell() helper function that calls exec.LookPath for bash and sh in order, returns first found or empty string. Add runtime import.
acceptance_criteria: sl init on Windows without bash/sh: post-init phase exits silently with no error. sl init on Windows with Git for Windows: init.sh executes via bash.exe. sl init on macOS/Linux: behavior unchanged (exec.Command(tmpFile.Name())).

title: Polish: run make test + make vet to verify no regressions on all fixes
description: WHY: Confirm all five fixes integrate correctly. No macOS/Linux regressions. All new and existing tests pass.
design: Run: make test (all unit + integration tests). Run: make vet. Run: make fmt (verify formatting). Run: make lint (if golangci-lint available). Fix any failures before marking complete.
acceptance_criteria: make test passes. make vet passes. make fmt produces no diffs. All existing integration tests in tests/integration/bootstrap_test.go pass.
